headline: MGM Resorts Jumps Above Buy Point, Leads S&P 500 As Analysts See Las Vegas Rebound
summary: MGM Resorts International gapped above a buy point Wednesday after two analysts upgraded the stock, citing improving Las Vegas trends.  MGM stock is the best-performer in the S&P 500 today.  JPMorgan Chase analysts led by Daniel Politzer told clients that growth should improve in the coming months vs. easier comparisons and as U.S. leisure travelers remain resilient.

headline: MGM Stock Receives Two Upgrades. Can the Stock Close At Its Highest Level Since 2024?
summary: Shares of  MGM Resorts International  surged on Wednesday, after two firms upgraded the stock based primarily on the belief that consumers will be flocking back to the Las Vegas Strip.  MGM stock jumped 8.2% to $41.60, placing it near the top of the  for the trading session—and putting shares on pace for their highest closing price since October 29, 2024, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  Broadly, the two upgrades were based on visitors and consumers returning to the Las Vegas Strip after two years of challenging dynamics.

MGM stock jumped 8.2% to $41.60, placing it near the top of the  for the trading session—and putting shares on pace for their highest closing price since October 29, 2024, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  Broadly, the two upgrades were based on visitors and consumers returning to the Las Vegas Strip after two years of challenging dynamics.

headline: JPMorgan upgrades MGM Resorts, sees 20% upside on rebounding Las Vegas demand
